data.title: Ralph Lauren's upbeat holiday boosts FQ3 results
data.description:
Shares of Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) are gaining ground in Thursday’s premarket trading as better-than-expected holiday sales for the retailer boosted its bottom-line by 16%.

"We are encouraged by this quarter's strong performance, and we continue to be sharply focused on what's ahead for Ralph Lauren: leveraging the incredible power of our brand and diverse drivers of growth to stay on offense into the next year and beyond,” said the company’s CEO Patrice Louvet.

Although foreign currency headwinds impacted sales, sales across all regions improved from a year ago with North American sales up 7%, European sales up 16%, and up by 14% across all Asian markets.

Driven by favorable product, channel and geographic mix, lower commodity costs and higher prices for its merchandise, all of which more than offset freight costs, gross margin for the quarter increased by 200 basis points to 68.4%.

In the fiscal third quarter, Ralph Lauren earned an adjusted profit of $4.82 per share, an increase of 15% from a year ago and $0.29 better than anticipated.

Taking into account the current geopolitical and macroeconomic environment, including inflationary pressures and U.S. trade policies, the company anticipates sales in FY25 to increase by 6% to 7% as foreign exchange headwinds will likely negatively impact sales by ~100 to 150 basis points.

Gross margin is expected to expand ~130-170 basis points with operating margin to expand ~120-160 basis points, both of which will be negatively impacted by FX headwinds by ~30-50 basis points.

In FQ4, gross margin is expected to expand 80-120 basis points with operating margin to expand 120-140 basis points, both of which are negatively impacted by FX headwinds by ~60-80 basis points.

Ralph Lauren (NYSE:RL) shares are higher by more than 12% in Thursday’s premarket trading.

data.title: AURI files to lower the company's authorized shares by 1B shares
data.description:
• AURI Inc. (OTCPK:AURI) said on Thursday that it is submitting correspondence to Wyoming's Department of State amending AURI's articles of incorporation, reducing the company's authorized shares by 1 billion shares.
  • The company's reduction in authorized shares should be effective within the next few weeks.
  • The company's transfer agent will adjust the company's records to reflect the reduction and will report the new authorized shares number to OTC Markets' issuer services division once Wyoming approves the amendment.

data.title: Runway Growth Capital leads $75 million loan as part of $120 million financing for Piano
data.description:
• Runway Growth Capital (NASDAQ:RWAY) on Thursday announced its role as the lead lender in a $75 million senior secured term loan facility as a part of a $120 million financing to Piano.
  • The financing also includes a $45 million Series D investment led by Updata Partners, a Washington, D.C.-based growth equity firm focused on technology.
  • The company said the loan will enable Piano to optimize its platform, expand its market presence, and support strategic growth initiatives.
  • RWAY +1.65% premarket to $11.7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Macom's stock rises after Q1, outlook surpasses expectations
data.description:
Shares of Macom Technology (NASDAQ:MTSI) rose about 5% premarket on Thursday after fiscal first quarter results and second quarter outlook beat estimates.

For the fiscal first quarter ended Jan. 3, the company generated $218.1M in revenue, an increase of 38.8% year-over-year. Macom — which supplies analog and radio frequency and other semiconductor products — saw adjusted EPS jump 36.2% year-over-year to $0.79. Both the top and bottom-line numbers exceeded analysts expectations.

“Q1 was a good start to our fiscal 2025,” said Macom's President and CEO Stephen Daly. “We remain focused on serving our customers and building a stronger, broader and more competitive product portfolio.”

For the fiscal second quarter, Macom expects revenue to be between $227M and $233M (with midpoint at $230M), compared to consensus revenue estimate of $223.59M.

The Lowell, Mass.-based company expects adjusted EPS to be in the range of $0.82 to $0.86 (midpoint at $0.84) versus consensus of $0.83.

Earlier in the week, peer Cirrus Logic (CRUS), also a supplier for Apple (AAPL) reported its fiscal third quarter results, which exceeded expectations.

Related stocks: Silicon Laboratories (SLAB) -1%, Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) -2%, Cirrus Logic (CRUS) -2%, Synaptics (SYNA) +1%, premarket on Thursday.

data.title: ConocoPhillips in Charts: Production rises across segments in Q4, amid falling crude oil realizations
Babcock & Wilcox subsidiary receives $35M contract
data.description:
• Babcock & Wilcox (B&W) announced that its Babcock & Wilcox Construction subsidiary was awarded $35M in contracts for maintenance and service work at power plants and industrial facilities in North America.
  • This brings the company's construction services backlog to over $160M as it enters 2025.
  • BW shares rise 4.2% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Stock index futures muted as job cuts jump, initial jobless claims come in hotter
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted on Thursday, and yields were largely flat as job cuts jumped in January over the prior month and initial jobless claims came in hotter.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were unchanged, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.21%.

Market sentiment took a hit after QUALCOMM (QCOM) and Ford (F) both fell over 4% after their earnings.

Wall Street's major market averages closed higher Wednesday, overcoming earlier pressure brought on in part by a slump in shares of Magnificent Seven tech heavyweight Alphabet.

"After a severe allergic reaction on Monday after the tariff news, markets continued to be relatively sedated yesterday as investors continued to price out the chance of aggressive tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

In terms of more detail on what drove the moves yesterday, the ISM services print added fuel to the bond rally, as the prices paid component fell back to 60.4, after spiking to 64.4 the previous month, Reid added.

Labor reports take the spotlight in today's economic calendar.

The number of job cuts announced by U.S. employers jumped 28% to 49,795 in January from December, but it fell 40% from January 2024, according to Challenger Gray & Christmas data.

Initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week.

The Q4 productivity and costs report will also be released before the market opens, and the Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

"U.S. productivity and unit labor cost data is important but not reliable in real time. Automation and technology should increase productivity over time, as will social trends," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Tech will once again be under the radar, with Amazon (AMZN) reporting its Q4 earnings after the bell.

data.title: Ladder Capital Non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 misses by $0.01, revenue of $68.61M beats by $2.34M
data.description:
• Ladder Capital press release (NYSE:LADR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.27 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $68.61M (-3.3% Q/Q) beats by $2.34M.

data.title: ArcelorMittal to build $900M steel plant in Alabama to meet U.S. auto sector demand
data.description:
ArcelorMittal (NYSE:MT) said Thursday it plans to build an advanced steel manufacturing facility in Calvert, Alabama, to raise its production capacity to meet demand from the U.S. automotive sector.

ArcelorMittal (NYSE:MT) said the plant, estimated to cost $900M, would be able to deliver up to 150 kt/year of premium non-grain-oriented electrical steel.

The company said demand for sophisticated NOES in the U.S. is rising while domestic supplies are limited, which should support a premium pricing outlook for NOES produced at the new plant.

"If you include our mines in Canada, ~40% of our EBITDA is generated in North America, so we will continue to look for options to develop the business in the region," CFO Genuino Christino said on the earnings conference call.

Christino estimated that potential tariffs would cause a ~$100M hit per quarter to the company, in line with analyst estimates, but if tariffs on Mexico are introduced, ArcelorMittal (MT) has the flexibility to reduce slab imports from there and increase them from Brazil instead.

ArcelorMittal (MT) +5.2% pre-market Thursday after reporting its Q4 net loss narrowed to $390M from a $2.97B loss in the year-earlier quarter when earnings were hurt by the disposal of its Kazakh operations and by an impairment charge related to Acciaierie d’Italia, and Q4 EBITDA jumped 14% Y/Y to $1.65B, compared with analyst estimates of a $319M net profit and $1.53B in EBITDA, according to a company-provided consensus.
